name: libwin-hivex-perl
title: libwin-hivex-perl
description:
Perl bindings to hivex
Perl bindings for libhivex, a library for reading and writing
Windows Registry "hive" binary files.
.
This package also contains hivexregedit, a low-level command-line
tool for manipulating and dumping registry hives.

commands.name: hivexregedit
commands.description: Merge and export Registry changes from regedit-format files.

arguments.description: debugging in the hivex library. This is useful for diagnosing bugs and also mal-

arguments.description: Use heuristics to tolerate certain levels of corruption within hives.
arguments.raw: --unsafe  Use heuristics to tolerate certain levels of corruption within hives.

arguments.raw: --unsafe-printable-strings  When exporting (only), assume strings are UTF-16LE and print them as strings instead of

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use hivexregedit because Perl bindings to hivex.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use hivexregedit as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hivexregedit --merge [--prefix prefix] [--encoding enc] \` as documented upstream for hivexregedit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hivexregedit --export [--prefix prefix] hivefile key > regfile` as documented upstream for hivexregedit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hivexregedit --export \` as documented upstream for hivexregedit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hivexregedit --merge system.hive \` as documented upstream for hivexregedit.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hivexregedit with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hivexregedit --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: hivexregedit - Merge and export Registry changes from regedit-format files.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install hivex (1.3.24-2.1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `hivexregedit --merge [--prefix prefix] [--encoding enc] \`. Further invocations from upstream: `hivexregedit --export [--prefix prefix] hivefile key > regfile`; `hivexregedit --export \`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl bindings to hivex.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: hivexregedit - Merge and export Registry changes from regedit-format files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install hivex (1.3.24-2.1)
